metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 'reuse attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file' assertion: either confirm that `dependency_hashes()` composes correctly with a code-review verdict (no ticket_id, different citation schema), or clarify that only `_hash_file` (the primitive) is reused and `code_review/region_gate.py` will build its own path-collection logic over the code-review verdict structure.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file' for the code-review content-hash map, but the actual `dependency_hashes()` function in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py` is tightly coupled to plan-review concerns: it reads `ticket file_impact` via `rebar.get_file_impact(ticket_id)`, resolves citation paths from plan-review verdict buckets (`blocking`/`advisory`/`coaching`/etc.), and resolves a pinned-SHA snapshot through the plan-review attested gate. Code review has no ticket_id in the same sense and its artifact structure differs. The only genuinely reusable piece is the private `_hash_file` helper, not `dependency_hashes` itself.
findings.location: Design section — 'store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map {path: sha256} in the artifact (reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the module/function that calls `contracts.register_contract('code_review_novelty', novelty_model)` (e.g. `code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`) and add it to the plan's scope/file list so the registration site is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan adds a new `code_review_novelty` output_schema and `code-novelty` reviewer id but does not name the module/site where `contracts.register_contract('code_review_novelty', novelty_model)` is called. In the existing plan-review path this registration lives in `plan_review/passes.py::register_contracts()`; for code-review, the analogous site is unspecified. Without the registration, `RunRequest(output_schema='code_review_novelty', ...)` fails at runtime with an unregistered-schema error, silently breaking the novelty sub-call.
findings.location: Design section: 'code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list) and a new reviewer id (code-novelty)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `contracts.register_contract('code_review_novelty', novelty_model)` to `src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py::register_contracts()`, import `novelty_model` from `review_kernel.verify`, and mention this site in the plan's Design section alongside the new reviewer prompt.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify `code_review_novelty.md`'s required template variables (e.g. `{{diff}}`, `{{findings}}`), its `outputs:` contract binding, its `execution_mode`/`category`, and whether the `cites_prior_location` NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS sub-question is adapted for source-file citations or reused verbatim.
findings.finding: The new `code_review_novelty.md` prompt (reviewer id `code-novelty`) is mandated by the plan but its required structural metadata is never specified: (a) which template variables it receives (plan_review_novelty.md uses `{{plan}}`; the code variant presumably needs `{{diff}}` and/or `{{findings}}`); (b) which `outputs:` schema it binds to (if `novelty` it reuses the kernel model, but the existing `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` sub-question `cites_prior_location` is plan-section-scoped language — the plan never specifies whether this sub-question is semantically adapted for source-file/line citations or reused verbatim); (c) `execution_mode` and `category` fields required by the reviewer index format.
findings.location: Design section — 'code review adds its own novelty reviewer prompt (code_review_novelty.md, oriented to a diff + a finding list) and a new reviewer id (code-novelty)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prompt-spec block to the Design section: template vars `{{diff}}` (the current diff) and `{{prior_findings}}` (the surfaced prior findings list); `outputs: novelty` (reusing the kernel contract); adapt `cites_prior_location` description to 'same source file/line region as that prior finding?' in the prompt body; set `execution_mode: single_turn`, `category: code-review-pass`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend c3e4's AC to include: 'If the audit finds attest.py feeds dropped findings as novelty priors, fix it within this story or create a tracked follow-on before this story closes.'
findings.finding: Child c3e4's AC includes 'Audit every novelty prior-set consumer (attest.py:441 also calls latest_review_result) to confirm none feeds dropped findings as novelty priors' — but if the audit finds attest.py does feed dropped findings, no child in the roster commits to fixing it. The AC says 'confirm none feeds,' implying a fix is required if the audit fails, but no child owns that fix. This is a potential owned-by-none gap for a real defect.
findings.location: child c3e4-7a6c-a4cc-4fb8, AC: 'Audit every novelty prior-set consumer (attest.py:441 also calls latest_review_result) to confirm none feeds dropped findings as novelty priors.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to c3e4 AC: 'Fix any consumers found to feed dropped findings as novelty priors, or escalate to a tracked follow-on with explicit ownership before closing this child.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the Design body's '(see AC5 / old-frilly-plankton)' cross-reference: either correct it to reference AC17 (c639) and AC1, or add a dedicated AC explicitly stating 'the novelty prior set passed to the scorer includes only findings with decision in {block, advisory}'.
findings.finding: The Design body cross-references 'AC5' to mean the surfaced-only constraint ('Novelty is scored ONLY against findings returned to the client (surfaced block/advisory), never against dropped/hidden findings (see AC5 / old-frilly-plankton)'), but the actual AC5 in the Acceptance Criteria list covers the local artifact title scheme ('Local artifacts use the "code-review: session:{session_id}" title scheme'), not the surfaced-only rule. The surfaced-only rule is not covered by any numbered AC (it is partially covered by AC1's 'SURFACED' qualifier and by AC17/c639).
findings.location: Design paragraph 'Novelty is scored ONLY...' vs Acceptance Criteria AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Change '(see AC5 / old-frilly-plankton)' to '(see AC1 / AC17 / c639)' and verify AC1's 'SURFACED (decision in {block, advisory})' qualifier is the covering criterion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the AC cross-reference in the first Success Criteria bullet: identify which AC (or add a new one) specifies the region_gated_floor convergence test harness, so the observable proof for 'prior low-priority surfaced finding is labeled carried_over and not re-coached' is unambiguously pointed to.
findings.finding: The Success Criteria paragraph references 'AC3' as the test harness for the observable convergence proxy, but AC3 in the Acceptance Criteria is 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id as a top-level field' — a schema AC, not a test harness. The actual convergence test harness is not given an AC number, so the cross-reference is wrong and could misdirect the implementer.
findings.location: Success Criteria, first bullet: 'verifiable by the region_gated_floor test harness (AC3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC such as 'A test harness verifies that, on a re-run with no cited-file changes, every prior low-priority surfaced finding is labeled carried_over, omitted from coaching, and not re-surfaced as novel' and reference that AC number in the Success Criteria bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC17 and AC18 inline: state the file/function to change, the observable behavior (e.g., 'reader filters the artifact findings list to entries where decision ∈ {block, advisory} before returning them'), and a one-line test description — replacing the bare tracker-ID references.
findings.finding: AC17 ('c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only') and AC18 ('Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only') use opaque external tracker IDs with no inline description of what code change is required or where it lives. An executor cannot act on either AC without first looking up the referenced items, which may not be accessible in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC17 and AC18
findings.suggested_fix: AC17: 'The code-review artifact reader (code_review/reader.py or equivalent) filters loaded findings to only those with decision ∈ {block, advisory} before returning them to the novelty seam; a unit test confirms dropped/hidden findings are excluded from the returned list.' AC18: 'The plan-review novelty loader (review_kernel/verify.py or equivalent) filters prior findings to decision ∈ {block, advisory}; a unit test confirms non-surfaced findings are excluded.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `_hash_file` is extracted to a shared utility (e.g., `review_kernel/hashing.py`) or imported directly from plan_review's `attest` module; if the latter, acknowledge and justify the cross-module coupling or add a mitigating interface boundary.
findings.finding: The plan reuses plan-review's internal `_hash_file` utility directly from `attest.dependency_hashes` without clarifying whether this is a shared-utility import or a cross-module dependency into plan_review's private implementation. If it is a direct import from plan_review, code_review gains a silent coupling to plan_review internals with no interface/event boundary, making future refactors of _hash_file break both gates silently.
findings.location: Design section: 'store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map … reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file'
findings.suggested_fix: Move `_hash_file` (and the sha256 hashing logic) to `review_kernel/hashing.py` so both plan_review and code_review import from a shared, stable location — eliminating the cross-module dependency while preserving reuse.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider whether old-frilly-plankton (a plan-review bug fix) should be a standalone ticket rather than a child of this code-review feature epic, since it has an independent reason-to-change and is releasable independently.
findings.finding: old-frilly-plankton is a bug fix in the plan-review novelty subsystem — a different gate and a different reason-to-change — bundled as a child of a code-review feature epic. It is independently valuable and releasable regardless of whether code-review memory ships.
findings.location: Child ticket: old-frilly-plankton / AC lines AC17-18

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an integration/E2E test in one child (c639 or e51b) that runs a local review (session-keyed), then runs a Gerrit-keyed review, and asserts the Gerrit verdict (not just the reader output) is unaffected by the prior local session artifact.
findings.finding: AC16 is only partially covered: the parent requires 'A test verifies a prior local review does not change a change's first Gerrit review verdict,' but no child plans an end-to-end or integration-level test that executes a local review, then executes a Gerrit-keyed review, and confirms the full verdict (not just the reader output) is unaffected. Child c639 plans a unit-level typed-key isolation test (session: key never returns change: artifact), which covers the mechanism but not the full verdict path the parent AC specifies.
findings.location: child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2, AC: 'typed-key isolation (a session: key never returns a change: artifact and vice versa)'; parent AC16
findings.suggested_fix: Extend e51b's test suite with a test that mocks a session-keyed artifact in the store, invokes the full region_gated_floor pipeline with a change-keyed lookup, and asserts no findings are dropped (full unfiltered set returned), confirming verdict-level isolation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a single child (c639 or 3bff) as the owner of the title-construction helper for local artifacts, and have the other child import/reuse it rather than re-implementing.
findings.finding: The local artifact title construction (the string 'code-review: session:{session_id}') is planned by BOTH c639 (payload/reader child) and 3bff (local persistence wiring child) without either child explicitly owning or delegating the title-assembly logic. If both children implement the title string independently in gate_dispatch.py or sidecar.py, the logic could diverge or be duplicated.
findings.location: child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2, AC: 'Local artifacts are titled code-review: session:{session_id}'; child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33, AC: 'emits a code_review artifact keyed session:<id>'
findings.suggested_fix: Define a module-level constant or small helper (e.g., code_review_local_title(session_id)) in c639's sidecar.py (since c639 owns the payload schema), and have 3bff's gate_dispatch.py wiring import and call it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC17 and AC18 as observable end-states with explicit verification: name the predicate/filter applied, the artifact field inspected, and the test that would be false before this work and true after.
findings.finding: AC17 and AC18 are effort-framed ('makes X surfaced-only') with no stated observable outcome or in-session verification method, making them unmeasurable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC17 and AC18

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Qualify SC1 to note that convergence applies only where a session lifecycle exists (i.e., resolve_session_id() returns a value), and bare `rebar review-code` without a session is intentionally inert, so test harnesses must use a valid session id to verify AC3.
findings.finding: Success Criteria SC1 states 'every prior low-priority SURFACED finding is labeled carried_over and NOT re-coached' as a general convergence claim, but the Design explicitly states local convergence is 'intentionally INERT' for bare `rebar review-code` invocations (uuid-fallback case). An implementer reading SC1 would expect convergence on all re-runs, while Design restricts it; the two sections contradict each other without qualification in the Success Criteria.
findings.location: Success Criteria (SC1) vs Design (uuid-fallback rationale paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to SC1: '(Convergence applies only where a session lifecycle exists — resolve_session_id() returns a non-None id. Bare `rebar review-code` with no session id mints a fresh uuid per invocation by design; the region_gated_floor test harness must supply a stable session id to exercise this criterion.)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence description in the plan body for each of c639 and old-frilly-plankton: what module/function is changed, what the before/after behavior is, and where the test lives — so AC17 and AC18 are self-contained and verifiable.
findings.finding: AC17 ('c639 makes the code-review reader surfaced-only') and AC18 ('Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only') reference external work items (c639, old-frilly-plankton) with no description of what those changes entail in the plan body — no described deliverable, scope, or file paths. These are orphan acceptance criteria: they cannot be verified from the plan's own text.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC17, AC18

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align terminology: decide whether the schema field is `carried_from` (a pointer to the prior run key) or `carried_over` (a boolean status flag), update Success Criteria and AC11 to use the same term, and if both concepts are needed, define both explicitly.
findings.finding: The Design uses 'carried_from' as the schema field name ('Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from') while the Success Criteria uses 'labeled carried_over' ('every prior low-priority SURFACED finding is labeled carried_over'). These appear to name the same concept but use different terms, creating a terminology inconsistency.
findings.location: Design (carryover schema field paragraph) vs Success Criteria (SC1)
findings.suggested_fix: If `carried_from` holds the prior run/patchset key (as Design states), then SC1 should read 'is stamped with carried_from' rather than 'labeled carried_over'; alternatively, add a separate boolean `carried_over: true` field and keep both terms with distinct meanings documented.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback / expand-contract note for the code_review_result_v1 schema extension (e.g., confirm new fields are additive-safe for all existing consumers, and describe the back-out path if rollback is needed post-deployment).
findings.finding: The plan extends code_review_result_v1 with two new top-level fields (session_id, {path:sha256} deps map) but provides no rollback path or expand-contract sequence if deployment needs to be reverted — old readers encountering a new artifact or new readers encountering an old artifact are handled at runtime (REGION_UNKNOWN fail-safe), but a deployment rollback would leave new-format artifacts in the store that the rolled-back reader doesn't understand.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2, AC3, AC4 / Design: 'store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line rollback note in the Design section: 'New fields are additive; readers encountering an artifact missing these fields resolve to REGION_UNKNOWN (existing fail-safe). Rollback: re-deploy old voter — new-format artifacts in the store are ignored by the old reader since it did not implement the reader path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit justification and rollback/back-out path for AC18 (old-frilly-plankton's plan-review novelty prior-set change), or move it to its own plan with the required compat analysis.
findings.finding: AC18 ('Bug old-frilly-plankton makes the plan-review novelty prior set surfaced-only') is listed as an acceptance criterion of this epic but describes a behavioral change to an existing system (plan-review novelty scoring) with no rollback path, migration step, or acknowledge-and-justify statement — it is treated as an incidental side-task rather than an explicit, justified breaking change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC18

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a deliverable to update ADR 0008 (or create a new ADR) covering the code-review novelty extension: typed keyspaces, region_gated_floor contract, uuid-fallback tradeoff, and the file-level/line-level versioning boundary.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural decision cluster (typed keyspaces session:<id> vs change:<id>, file-level vs line-level region granularity, uuid-fallback convergence tradeoff, the region_gated_floor contract) that extends the existing ADR 0008 novelty mechanism to a new gate — but no ADR update or new ADR is listed as a deliverable.
findings.location: Design section / 'Keying + addressing' / 'uuid-fallback rationale'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or deliverable to create/update docs/session-id-shims.md to cover the REBAR_SESSION_ID shim pattern in the context of code-review local convergence.
findings.finding: The plan references docs/session-id-shims.md as the documentation for configuring REBAR_SESSION_ID for harnesses lacking a native session variable, but this document is not listed as a deliverable — if it does not already exist, users needing local convergence have no guidance; if it exists but is silent on code-review, it is now incomplete.
findings.location: Design section: 'SessionStart shim exporting REBAR_SESSION_ID (docs/session-id-shims.md)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'docs/session-id-shims.md exists and covers code-review artifact seeding via REBAR_SESSION_ID for harnesses lacking a native session variable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or design note specifying that a new ADR (or a documented extension of ADR 0008) will be created covering the code-review convergence design: content-hash region gate, REGION_UNKNOWN fail-safe, typed keyspace isolation, and uuid-fallback semantics.
findings.finding: This plan introduces a novel architectural decision — content-hash-based region gating for cross-patchset code-review memory, typed keyspace isolation (session vs change), and uuid-fallback convergence semantics — but names no ADR to be created or updated for this design. ADR 0008 covers plan-review convergence; the code-review equivalent is a meaningfully different design (file-hash gate, REGION_UNKNOWN fail-safe, local vs Gerrit namespace isolation) that warrants its own ADR or a documented extension of ADR 0008.
findings.location: Design section (whole); Success Criteria; no mention of ADR creation anywhere in the plan

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document whether new payload fields (session_id, deps map, carried_from) are backward-compatible additions or require a schema version bump, and acknowledge any existing consumers that must be updated or that must tolerate the new fields.
findings.finding: The plan extends code_review_result_v1 with new top-level fields (session_id, {path:sha256} deps map, carried_from per finding) but does not acknowledge existing consumers of this payload or describe backward-compatibility handling (e.g., whether new fields are optional/additive or require a schema version bump).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC3, AC4, AC11; Design section 'Keying + addressing'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete user-facing or operational validation mechanism (e.g., a dogfooding step on a known Gerrit change with repeated findings, or a before/after count of surfaced findings across two consecutive runs of the same change with no code edits) to the Success Criteria.
findings.finding: The plan's only named validation mechanism for the user-facing benefit ('repeat noise reduced') is an internal unit/integration test harness (region_gated_floor test). No before/after workflow comparison, operational metric, or dogfooding criterion is specified, making it impossible to verify that real users experience less repeated-finding noise in practice.
findings.location: Success Criteria, first bullet; Acceptance Criteria overall

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the `{path: sha256}` deps map covers (a) all files in the reviewed diff or (b) only files cited in surfaced findings — and confirm it matches the `attest.dependency_hashes` analogue (citations-only, since code review has no `file_impact` field) so the region gate is per-finding-precise rather than over-broad on large diffs.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify which files are included in the '{path: sha256}' region-hash map — specifically whether it covers (a) all files touched by the reviewed diff, or (b) only the files cited in surfaced findings. These produce materially different gate behavior: choice (a) marks REGION_CHANGED for a finding citing file X whenever ANY of the diff's files change, even if X itself is untouched — making the region gate over-broad on large diffs and defeating convergence on reviews that touch many files with few findings. Choice (b) (per-finding citation-level) is both more precise and the natural analogue of how attest.dependency_hashes works (citations UNION file_impact). The plan references reusing _hash_file / dependency_hashes but those operate on cited files; the implementer has no unambiguous basis to choose.
findings.location: Design section — 'Region gate is content-addressed and resilient…store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map {path: sha256} in the artifact'; AC2 'The reader returns the reviewed-file content-hash map from the resolved artifact'; AC4 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries a {path: sha256} deps map'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design section, add: 'The deps map contains exactly the union of file paths cited (kind=file) in surfaced (block/advisory) findings from that run — mirroring how attest.dependency_hashes builds its set from citations UNION file_impact, with file_impact being empty for a diff-scoped code review. Per-finding region state is computed by looking up each finding's cited path(s) in the map; a finding with no file citation resolves to REGION_UNKNOWN.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note in the Design section (and/or the new ADR) clarifying that the region gate conditions the Pass-3 *drop predicate only* — Pass-1 recall remains full — and is therefore architecturally distinct from the 'per-diff-region scoping' ADR 0008 rejected (which would have restricted Pass-1 scope).
findings.finding: The plan's novel region-gate pattern (REGION_UNCHANGED/CHANGED/UNKNOWN as a third conjunct on the drop predicate at Pass-3) superficially conflicts with ADR 0008's 'Alternatives Rejected' section, which explicitly rejects 'per-diff-region scoping' — but ADR 0008's rejection targets restricting Pass-1 RECALL scope, not conditioning a Pass-3 drop predicate. The plan never articulates this architectural distinction, leaving implementers and future reviewers with an apparent contradiction in the project's ADR record.
findings.location: Design section — 'Region gate is content-addressed and resilient'; ADR 0008 Alternatives Rejected: 'Per-diff-region scoping'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Design section, after 'Region gate is content-addressed and resilient...', add: 'Note: ADR 0008 rejected per-diff-region scoping because restricting Pass-1 to changed sections would break full recall. The region gate here is applied only at Pass-3 (the drop predicate), not Pass-1 — Pass-1 still runs all criteria over the full diff, so the recall guarantee is preserved. The region gate conditions only whether a novel+low-priority finding is dropped, not whether it is found.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit post-rollback schema-state assertion: state that (a) new fields are purely additive and old readers ignore unknown fields gracefully, OR (b) specify a compensating-forward procedure if old readers cannot tolerate new-format artifacts — and confirm no data loss on partial rollback.
findings.finding: The plan introduces new schema fields on `code_review_result_v1` (session_id, deps map, carried_from) but does not assert the schema state after a rollback of this feature. Artifacts written with the new format would persist in the store; the plan does not state whether the old reader handles these gracefully or whether any compensating action is needed.
findings.location: Design section — 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from'; AC3, AC4, AC11; 'Keying + addressing' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Schema evolution & rollback' note to the Design section: 'New fields (session_id, deps_hashes, carried_from) are purely additive; absent fields on old artifacts return None/empty-dict, which the reader treats identically to a load error (REGION_UNKNOWN, full surfacing). Post-rollback, new-format artifacts in the store are inert — old code ignores unknown fields. No data loss path exists.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly extend the `drop_reason` closed vocabulary to include `"novelty-region"` in the Design section and note that ADR 0024's vocabulary definition and any sidecar slim-payload reader must be updated accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `drop_reason: "novelty-region"` as a new value for the dropped-finding `drop_reason` field, but ADR 0024 formally specifies this as a closed vocabulary `drop_reason ∈ {null, "completion", "novelty"}`. The plan never amends the vocabulary definition, the ADR, or the sidecar/reader code that reads this field — leaving the schema in a contradictory state where the plan's new value is undocumented in the authoritative source.
findings.location: Design section — 'Dropped novel-on-unchanged findings move to the verdict dropped bucket with drop_reason "novelty-region"'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the coaching-skip mechanism for `carried_from` findings: whether they are pre-filtered from the coach LLM input list, post-filtered from coach output, or both, and at which call-site in the code review pipeline this filter is applied.
findings.finding: The plan mandates that carryover findings are 'omitted from coaching while remaining surfaced' (AC12), but never specifies the mechanism by which the coaching pass skips them: whether `carried_from` is used as a pre-filter before the coach LLM call (findings never sent to the coach prompt), a post-filter (coach output discarded for these findings), or whether the coaching prompt needs a guard against re-coaching carried findings it might still receive. The processing protocol is stated as a rule but not co-located with the coaching schema or pass definition.
findings.location: Design section — 'Carryover findings carry an explicit schema field carried_from ... are OMITTED from coaching (not re-coached); they remain surfaced.' / AC12
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Design: 'Findings with `carried_from` set are pre-filtered from the coaching pass input list before the coach LLM call; the coach never receives them, so no post-filter is needed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace legacy ticket slugs in e51b's Scope dependency declarations with the canonical child IDs (c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 for reader/payload; 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33 for local persistence).
findings.finding: Child e51b uses legacy ticket slugs ('revenued-thickset-dassie' for c639, 'paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish' for 3bff) to name its inter-child dependencies, which are non-navigable within the plan's child roster that uses UUID-style IDs. These residual references create a traceability gap: a reader cannot resolve the dependency chain without external knowledge of the slug-to-ID mapping.
findings.location: child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06, Scope section: 'Depends on the reader/payload (revenued-thickset-dassie) and, for end-to-end LOCAL integration, on local persistence (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish)'
findings.suggested_fix: In e51b Scope, replace 'Depends on the reader/payload (revenued-thickset-dassie)' with 'Depends on c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 (reader/payload)' and 'for end-to-end LOCAL integration, on local persistence (paradoxal-balsamic-bubblefish)' with 'for end-to-end LOCAL integration, on 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33 (local persistence)'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
